content: The human language classroom definition of reflexion as both 'reflection of external input' and 'intentional personal反思 (self-reflection)' can inform the design of better LLM self-reflection mechanisms: just as language learners use reflexion to connect new vocabulary to their existing lived experiences, LLMs can be designed to ground self-reflection not just in evaluating output correctness, but in 'reflecting' how their generated output aligns with the original context and intent of the user's input.
